common.noRegistration: No sign-up · Data stays on your device

welcome.headline:
Motivation is not
a feeling.
welcome.headlineSub:
One action per day, honest check-in,
reconnect when you fall.

0.title: One clear action
0.sub: Zero decision fatigue every day — the card tells you what to do.

1.title: Anchor system
1.sub: Attach to an existing routine; no need to carve out new time.

2.title: Built for recovery
2.sub: Never punishes you for falling. Helps you reconnect.

step1.title:
Who do you want
to be in 66 days?
step1.sub: Choose an identity or write your own. You'll attach it to an anchor and time in the next step.
step1.infoBody: There may not be an exact match in the list — choose the closest one. You'll refine the details with your anchor and habit name.
step1.customBlurb: Write a custom statement; you'll set the anchor and time in the next step.
step1.customPlaceholder: E.g. Someone who drinks water daily and takes care of themselves…

step2.title:
Where will you
anchor this identity?
step2.sub: An anchor is what locks your daily suggestions to real life. Add it to a routine you already do.
step2.commitmentPlaceholder: Your commitment sentence will appear here when you choose an anchor…
step2.infoText: The template only gives the first suggestion; you can change the anchor and time whenever you want.
step2.templateHint: anchor pre-selected, change if you like.

step3.title:
Why did you want
this identity?
step3.sub: Even one sentence is enough. We save this motivation — to remind you on tough days.
step3.placeholder: E.g. I want to feel more energetic…
step3.systemTitle: What does this system do for you?

promises.0: Suggests 1 action daily — zero thinking, zero decision fatigue.
promises.1: Reads momentum and effort signals to choose your personalised move.
promises.2: Intervenes when it detects a drop. Never breaks streaks, never punishes.

step3.notifBody: Used for morning guidance and evening reminders. You can turn it off in settings.

premium.headline: Personal Discipline Programme
premium.narrative1: You're not paying for an app.
premium.narrativeEmphasis: You're making a decision for yourself.
premium.narrative2: You're starting a process you'll take seriously.
premium.optOut: Premium is not required — continuing for free is perfectly fine; you can choose at any time.
premium.commitmentEffect: People who invest are generally 2–3× more likely to keep going (commitment effect).
premium.extensionNote: If the expected automaticity doesn't form, you can request an extension via support.
premium.dismissSub: Keep using the app for free
premium.purchaseError: Purchase could not be completed. Try again or restore.
premium.restoreSuccessBody: Your subscription has been successfully restored. You can access all premium features.
premium.restoreNotFound: No active subscription found. Check which account you used to purchase.
premium.restoreError: Restore failed. Check your internet and store account.
benefits: 66-day journey map — tap each day for full summary
Daily coach pack: principle · science · take action
Identity Mirror — reflects you from your mind notes
Weekly SDT survey + phase training (4 cards · ~30 sec)
Unlimited Mind notes · tomorrow plan always free

triggers.journey: Tomorrow plan is always free. With Premium: 66-day journey map, tap each day for full summary, Identity Mirror, weekly SDT survey, phase training and today's coach pack (principle + science + action).
triggers.day7: Day 7: the journey map fully opens. With Premium you can see all 66 days, tap each day for that day's check-in, plan and coach summary. Phase training and Identity Mirror are waiting for you.
triggers.day22: You're in the middle of day 22 — the habit is starting to solidify. Full journey map, 3 phase history, full day summary on tap and Identity Mirror to run this consciously.
triggers.profile: Premium: deepen your journey with the 66-day map, Identity Mirror, weekly SDT survey, phase training and daily coach pack (principle + science + action).

home.profileErrorBody: Storage may have been temporarily unreadable due to missing permissions. You can try again.
home.fiveDefaultTrigger: Take a deep breath, drop your shoulders — then make the one clear move for your habit.

card.noAnchor: {{title}} · a small touch on your {{habit}} path.

modal.recoveryHint: Your pace. One small move is enough.

doneSubline.activation: Your start reflex got one rep stronger.
doneSubline.focus: You gathered attention in one place.
doneSubline.recovery: A small step reopens the path.

_fallback.step2: Take the smallest physical step for {{habit}}

anchor-visualize.lead_sleep_winddown: Before bed: «{{anchor}}» — picture tomorrow's first move.
anchor-visualize.step1: Repeat the anchor aloud or silently
anchor-visualize.step2: Finish the first {{habit}} move in your mind

anchor-touch.step1: Touch the anchor object or spot once

mark-done-intent.step1: Intent line aloud or in a whisper
mark-done-intent.step2: Go to the anchor and start the first move

habit-whisper.step2: Do the smallest move at the anchor

say-identity.step1: Say who you are in one sentence

two-minute.step1: Start a 2-minute box in your head
two-minute.step2: Do the small version where the anchor happens

checkIn.streakGreat: {{count}} days in a row — you're doing great

plans.emptyTodayBody: The list you wrote in Journey yesterday appears here. You can set the first plan for tomorrow.
plans.tomorrowHint: Add 1 main + max 2 support items for a clear anchor tomorrow morning.
plans.planHint: Check off or confirm the day with check-in.
plans.planHintShort: Tap to check off · lock the day with check-in

day1.title: The process continues
day1.body: Missing 1 day is about 1.5% of the 66-day path. The neural pathway is open — a small repeat in the same direction with today's card is enough.

day2.title: Time to scale down
day2.body: Skipping two days in a row points to an over-ambitious goal. Scale the behaviour to 2 minutes, keep the same anchor; choose the small version on today's card and do an honest check-in.

day3.title: Reconnect
day3.body: You've disconnected for a few days — this doesn't mean your brain forgot the path. Lally's curve varies widely between individuals: scale down, refresh the trigger/anchor; choose the smallest clear step from today's card and restart with a real check-in.

actionGate.strictBody: Complete today's suggested step before confirming the day. It's short — for momentum.
actionGate.softBody: Check-in builds the habit; but a 30-second move genuinely primes the brain.
actionGate.strictHint: Strict mode on — you can turn it off in Profile → Advanced preferences.

firstWeek.title: First week — small map
firstWeek.body: The app is dense; these five small steps settle the initial feel.
firstWeek.stepCheckIn: Check-in or action from Today's box
firstWeek.stepAction: At least one live action (from card)
firstWeek.stepMind: Write even one line in the Mind tab
firstWeek.stepTomorrow: Write a main item for tomorrow in Journey

autoTrend.title: Last 7 days · automaticity
autoTrend.empty: No data yet. Rate after check-in — your chart builds here in ~10 seconds.

insight.fluent: Latest day felt fluent ({{score}}/10) — habit is settling in.
insight.medium: Latest day was medium ({{score}}/10) — small reps still count.
insight.hard: Latest day felt hard ({{score}}/10) — shrink the step, keep the anchor.

automaticityRating.title: Today's evaluation
automaticityRating.subtitle: Honest answers reflect your progress best.
automaticityRating.reminder: Skipping is fine. But if you don't log automaticity, your progress chart won't build; most days, a few seconds on this step is enough to see automation by day 66.
automaticityRating.autoHint: 1: I thought and decided · 10: Fully automatic
automaticityRating.effortQuestion: How much strength / effort did it take?
automaticityRating.warning: The behaviour felt automatic but took a lot of effort. Does the habit feel too big? Consider scaling back to a tiny version.

behavior.prediction: Estimated ~{{days}} days to automaticity — every step gets you closer.

journey.subtitle: 66-day identity build · Tomorrow plan + map · Day {{day}}

locked.title: Premium journey panel
locked.body: 66-day map, Identity Mirror, SDT pulse, phase training (4 cards) and a daily coach pack. Tomorrow plan is always free.

phaseTeaser.kicker: How does the brain work in this phase?

coach.brainTitle: How does the brain work in this phase?
coach.brainDone: You've completed this phase summary before — open to refresh.
coach.brainCards: 4 short cards · ~30 sec · tailored to your phase
coach.sentenceSub: One line in Mind in writing order · want to go to full Mind

sdt.title: Motivation pulse (SDT)
sdt.sub: Autonomy, competence, relatedness — weekly mini measurement

regression.title: Automaticity curve
regression.body: Estimated slope from first 14 days: +{{slope}} / day

survey.title: Weekly survey
survey.sub: Select a value between 1 (not at all) and 5 (very much) based on how you felt this week.

tomorrowPlan.bridgeBody: Today's check-in and micro steps align with this list. Leave a clear anchor for tomorrow morning.
tomorrowPlan.addMeta: You can add a time or trigger
tomorrowPlan.limitHint: 3 items is enough — keep it simple.
tomorrowPlan.emptyTitle: Build a short list for tomorrow
tomorrowPlan.emptySub: Pick one main micro-step. Morning reminder arrives; check-in on the Today screen.

mapTeaser.hint: Tomorrow plan is always free. Tap the active phase — short preview; full day summary and map are premium.

1.label: Foundation
1.subtitle: Tiny Habits kick in. Set your anchor, stay small.

2.label: Consolidation
2.subtitle: Identity shift begins. From 'someone who does' to 'someone who is'.

3.label: Automaticity
3.subtitle: The neural path completes. You do it without spending willpower.

history.emptyBody: Write a sentence every day. Getting thoughts out opens space in your brain — without effort, without judgement.

dump.title: Mind Dump
dump.desc: Write when your head is full, relax. What you write stays only on this device — tone analysis lightly feeds the behaviour engine.

modal.title: 📝 Mind Dump
modal.subtitle: Write, save, relax
modal.placeholder: Write whatever comes to mind. Nobody will read it. You won't be judged.
modal.footerNote: 🕐 Automatically saved a few seconds after you stop typing.
modal.moodAction: You're in {{muscle}} mode — "{{action}}" could get you moving.
modal.emptySubtitle: Start writing your first thought. Nobody will read it.
modal.historyHint: Tap to open. Long press the row to delete, then tap the bin.
modal.filterEmpty: No Journey lines found; close the filter to return to all notes.
modal.deleteMessage: This note will be permanently deleted. This action cannot be undone.

profile.languageSubtitle: Interface language changes immediately, including daily principles, prompts, and journey cards.

settings.title: SETTINGS
settings.themeAlertMsg: Only light theme is supported at the moment.

footer.quote: Motivation is not a feeling, it's an action.
footer.aboutAlertMsg: A habit coach that works on local principles.

modal.notifHint: Main reminder time: {{time}}. Also use «Check notification permission from system» for permissions.

notifSuccess.title: Notifications on
notifSuccess.msg: Morning reminders will be scheduled according to your chosen time.

notifSettings.title: Permission required
notifSettings.msg: You need to enable notification permission in system settings so we can schedule notifications.

deleteAll.title: Delete all data
deleteAll.msg: Your habit history, check-in records and mind notes will be deleted from this device. This action cannot be undone.

error.title: Could not read profile
error.body: There may be a temporary issue with storage or permissions. You can try again to reload the profile.

advanced.contextHint: Context cue under the card; no AI reading you as a person.
advanced.restHint: Pushes are lighter until this date; it lifts automatically (or cancel early).
advanced.backupHint: One file you can keep in Drive / Files. Import overwrites data from the chosen file.
advanced.pasteModalHint: Open the .json in a text editor, copy all, paste here, then tap Apply.
advanced.pastePlaceholder: Paste full backup JSON here (schemaVersion, profile, …)
advanced.restoreSuccess: Backup applied (v1 or v2); records updated.
advanced.exportFail: Could not create the file or open share. Check permissions and try again.
advanced.pickerUnavailableMsg:
Expo Go or an outdated build may lack the document picker.

• Full fix: rebuild — npm run android / npm run ios.

• Quick option: copy backup .json and use «Restore by pasting».
advanced.importFail: Could not open or read the file.
advanced.importConfirmMsg: Current data will be replaced by the JSON you choose (cannot undo). Continue?
advanced.pasteConfirmMsg: Current data will be replaced by the JSON you paste (cannot undo).
advanced.pasteInvalid: Not valid JSON, or the file was copied incompletely.

commitment.title: Habit & anchor
commitment.labelWhy: Why do you want this path?
commitment.placeholderHabit: e.g. I am someone who moves every day
commitment.placeholderAnchor: e.g. After I finish my coffee
commitment.hint: Changing the identity template requires re-selecting in onboarding; here you update your daily wording.

goalProgress.title: 66-day journey
goalProgress.completed: 🎯 66 days complete. This is now part of who you are.

weeklySummary.title: Weekly summary
weeklySummary.slipHint: Recently, slips often cluster near {{day}} (simple on-device count; no server).
weeklySummary.consistentHint: You look steady this week — keep one clear action flow.
weeklySummary.noTimestamp: Completed days in this window have no time stamp (older entries); no time-of-day summary.
weeklySummary.peak: Most common time of day in completions: {{part}}.
weeklySummary.partialTimestamp: Some completions lack time; summary is an approximate local snapshot.

story.miniHint: Add daily ratings for an automaticity line
story.shareNote: Only the text you share is used; nothing is sent to a server.
story.shareMessage: Day {{day}}. {{pct}}% consistency. Still early, but the system is in place. #66DayDiscipline

privacy.title: Your data & privacy
bullets: All personal data (profile, check-in history, Mind notes) stays on this device; nothing is sent to a server.
No account email — the app is tied to the device. Losing the phone or deleting the app may erase data permanently; no cloud backup.
Delete data removes everything from this device; cannot be undone.
Do now suggestions use no reading AI; rule-based signals from selected word patterns.
privacy.foot: Premium purchases are billed via your store (Apple/Google); payment details are not stored by kimlik-app.

coachCard.title: Today's principle
coachCard.premiumNote: The map, Identity Mirror and SDT pulse are waiting for you in the Journey tab.

app.profileLoadFailed: Profile could not be read from this device
app.profileLoadFailedBody: Storage or permissions may have temporarily caused an issue. Data is not lost; you can try again.

open-window.title: Open a window, breathe fresh air for 5 sec

stand-tall.title: Stand tall, open your chest for 3 sec

tap-desk.title: Tap the desk twice, then move

light-on.title: Turn on a light or open the curtain

door-threshold.title: Go to the doorway, step inside

say-identity.title: Say who you are out loud

one-line-plan.title: Write today's one step in a line

tool-ready.title: Put the tool on the desk (book, mat, water…)

smallest-version-say.title: Say the smallest version out loud

identity-nod.title: Say “I'm someone who does this” and nod

mark-done-intent.title: Say “I'm doing it now” and go to your anchor

five-countdown.title: Count 5-4-3-2-1 and make the first move

shoulders-drop.title: Drop your shoulders consciously for 5 sec

unclench-jaw.title: Relax your jaw, tongue on the palate

stop-scroll-thumb.title: Lift your thumb off the screen for 3 sec

feet-flat-press.title: Press feet into the floor for 5 sec

screen-blank.title: Lift your head from the screen

blink-reset.title: Blink 10 times, then focus on one point

desk-clear-one.title: Remove one thing from the desk

read-one-word.title: Read the first word of your task

stare-one-point.title: Stare at a wall/point for 10 sec

headphones-on.title: Put on headphones or go silent

pen-cap-off.title: Uncap your pen, ready to write

single-task-say.title: Say your single task out loud

smile.title: Smile at yourself in the mirror

gentle-neck-roll.title: Tilt your neck gently to one side

forgive-yesterday.title: Say “Yesterday is done, today is here”

hand-on-heart.title: Hand on chest, breathe for 5 sec

step-outside.title: Go to the door, look outside 5 sec

tpl-clear-mind-write.title: Reduce your mind to one sentence

tpl-clear-mind-open.title: Open Mind tab, let the cursor blink

tpl-clear-list-one.title: Pick one of 3 things in your head, write it

tpl-clear-worry-out.title: Dump the worry on paper/note, close it

tpl-learn-first-line.title: Open the source, look at the first line

tpl-learn-name.title: See the source name and close

tpl-learn-page-turn.title: Turn a page or scroll forward

tpl-self-water.title: Put a glass of water on the desk, take a sip

tpl-self-glass.title: Just put the glass on the desk

tpl-self-wash-face.title: Splash face with cold water 5 sec

tpl-creator-open.title: Open the file, move cursor to first line

tpl-creator-word.title: Write or draw just one word

tpl-creator-idea.title: Write the one idea in your head as a sentence

tpl-creator-rough-line.title: Write one draft line, no editing

tpl-focus-task.title: Say your one current task out loud

tpl-focus-notify-off.title: Turn off notifications or focus mode

tpl-focus-desk-one.title: Leave only one work tool on the desk

tpl-sleep-screen.title: Turn off screen, close eyes 10 sec

tpl-sleep-phone.title: Put phone away, turn your face

tpl-sleep-dim-light.title: Dim or turn off the light

tpl-sleep-gratitude.title: Whisper one good thing from today

tpl-social-name.title: Write the name of someone on your mind

tpl-social-smile.title: Pause with intent to smile at next person

tpl-social-send-hi.title: Type “hi” or “how are you”, sending optional

micro-feet.title: Press feet into floor, hold 3 sec

micro-posture.title: Pull shoulders back, open chest 2 sec

micro-sip.title: Take a sip if you have water

micro-window.title: Look out a window or into the distance 5 sec

micro-name-habit.title: Say your habit name out loud

micro-one-word.title: Write today's intent in one word

micro-shoes-on.title: Put on or pick up your shoes

micro-door.title: Walk to the door, step on the threshold

micro-smile-mirror.title: Look at camera or mirror 2 sec

micro-phone-away.title: Put phone one arm's length away

micro-habit-start.title: Do the smallest part of your habit

micro-reset-breath.title: Breathe in nose, out mouth (3 times)

reason.interrupt: Drop detected. One move is enough: {{title}}
reason.mindDumpMulti: In recent notes "{{hint}}" keeps echoing — now: {{title}}.
reason.highEffort: Yesterday was {{effort}}/10 tough. Today we scale down: {{title}}
reason.coldStart: First days: a small, clear step. {{title}}
reason.declining: The path felt hard lately. A soft jump: {{title}}
reason.improving: Recent days went well. Keep this rhythm: {{title}}
reason.momentumDown: Down for 2 days. We restart now: {{title}}

situation.home: Context · home: even the smallest version counts.
situation.work: Context · work break: 1–2 min clear move.
situation.travel: Context · travel: one micro-step that fits where you are.

clear_mind.title: Someone with a clear mind
clear_mind.identityStatement: I am someone with a clear mind who knows their priorities.

mirror.lowEnergy: As someone with a clear mind, opening the Mind tab today is enough. The system has your back.
mirror.resistance: Someone with a clear mind turns resistance into writing. 10 seconds, one sentence.
mirror.identity: From clutter to clarity. Your mind now recognizes space.

phases.phase_1: Foundation — Write what's in your head.

moving_person.title: Someone who moves
moving_person.identityStatement: I am someone who respects their body and moves every day.

mirror.lowEnergy: As someone who moves, put on your shoes or take 10 steps today.
mirror.resistance: When resistance hits, someone who moves scales down — 1 rep is enough.
mirror.identity: Movement is part of who you are. Your body is waiting.

learner.title: Lifelong learner
learner.identityStatement: I am someone who learns a little every day.

phases.phase_2: Consolidation — One page at a time.

self_care.title: Someone who takes care of themselves
self_care.identityStatement: I am someone who takes small care of themselves every day.

creator.title: Creator
creator.identityStatement: I am someone who creates, even in small doses.

focused_worker.title: Deep focus person
focused_worker.identityStatement: I am someone who protects focus and does one thing at a time.

night_owl.title: Night rest person
night_owl.identityStatement: I am someone who winds down and protects sleep.

social_builder.title: Connection builder
social_builder.identityStatement: I am someone who nurtures connection in small ways.

1.principle: Getting started is hundreds of times more important than getting perfect.
1.science: Open tasks (Zeigarnik): the action initiated pushes the mind to shut down.
1.action: Download the habit to the smallest version today and start it only once.

2.principle: Motivation is emotion; emotions pass, the system remains.
2.science: Self-Determination Theory: sustainable behavior requires autonomy and meaning (Deci & Ryan).
2.action: Write down your reason for 10 seconds, then just start at the trigger.

4.science: Implementation intentions increase planned trigger-action pair success (Gollwitzer).

5.principle: Instinct: disintegrating. Solution: designing the environment.
5.science: Behavioral will: difficulty decreases when reducing friction in the environment (Thaler & Sunstein tendencies).
5.action: Prepare the tool/preparation in 30 seconds in advance in the evening.

6.principle: Identity is first the sentence, then the movement.
6.science: Self-schema: consistent story about behaviors reinforces identity (BEM/ identity reflection).

7.principle: About 7 days is the first signal sufficient to open a new pathway to the brain.
7.science: Synaptic plasticity is strengthened by repetition; duration varies between individuals (Lally variation).
7.action: Go back to the mini version from day one and complete another round.

8.principle: The thought of giving up is the brain's defense of the old way.
8.science: Old habit: basal ganglia-dopamine cycle; new pathway is still weak (habit research).
8.action: Positive detail: say out loud yesterday's 1 win.

9.principle: Sometimes the reward is just a 'done' feeling.
9.science: Reinforcement: complementary small rewards reinforce unity with dopamine; exaggeration.
9.action: Give yourself a tiny, healthy 'sign reward' right after completion.

10.science: Starting limit effect: small repetition is enough for growth; jumping breaks motivation.
10.action: Instead of time or volume, just focus on the 'is there' box.

11.principle: Stress is not goodwill, but demand that transcends the system.
11.science: Cognitive load: procedural memory is suppressed when the worker fills the memory (baddeley models).
11.action: Cut today's version to no more than 2 minutes.

12.principle: The first quarter is the most expensive; the fee is no longer payable.
12.science: Late start is more expensive to restore falling self-efficacy.
12.action: Set the exact time limit for today's action: timer.

13.principle: Friction is bad design, not bad will.
13.science: Media engineering changes the behavior that designs the defaults well (choice architecture).
13.action: Move bad triggers 1 step away (e.g. phone is in another room).

14.principle: Exposure to repetition leads to increased interest.
14.science: The mere exposure makes the familiar selectable.
14.action: Make the same micro-action intentionally boring: monotony switches to automation.

15.principle: Community is a different brain mode than solitude.
15.science: Social facilitation can slightly increase motivation in moderate difficulties; audience/support makes a difference.
15.action: Tell someone your intentions in one sentence (micro-accountability).

16.principle: Time depends on the diameter, not the source.
16.science: Implication intentions double execution by fixing time (meta-analyses).
16.action: Put a 5-minute nest in the calendar and let it ring (notification or peer).

17.principle: Procrastination is not laziness; it is delayed healing.
17.science: Difficulties with regulation create cognitive barriers; self-compassion does not exacerbate the label.
17.action: Without judging procrastination, just 'now 30 s' of action.

18.principle: Not once in a while: either trigger or postpone.
18.science: If-then plans direct involuntary responses (Gollwitzer, implementation intentions).

19.principle: Being bored is the tunnel to automation.
19.science: The automaticity curve averages 66+ days of variation; boredom is a process (Lally).
19.action: Same repetition; variation only environment (e.g. 1 rep in different room).

20.principle: Boundaries are not a fence to freedom, but a framework.
20.science: Restricted space reduces decision fatigue; self-management persists in the classroom (Baumeister).
20.action: Ceil today's maximum time in 2 minutes (know the end, not the time spent).

21.principle: In the third week, he starts to carry the sentence 'I do'.
21.science: Repetition strengthens procedural learning; 21 days have no magic, but the signal accumulates (popular myth vs science: duration varies in the individual).
21.action: Add 'total X repetitions to date' to the list, really write a number.

22.principle: When the installation phase is over, it's not about magnifying, it's about staying in the mirror.
22.science: The phase transition creates a storm of motivation; coherence must be maintained (habit research).
22.action: Swear by smallness: tomorrow, stay equal to or less than the version from phase 1 to day 1.

23.principle: Motivation will fall; it's part of the plan, not the breakdown.
23.science: The automaticity curve is slow and wavy (Lally and team, 2009).
23.action: Don't measure motivation; just focus on maintaining 1 iteration today.

24.principle: Identity, first sentence; then, many days again.
24.science: Identity-based habits (clear) are compatible with neuroscience: self-concept drives behavior (self-schemes).
24.action: Enlarge the sentence 'I am... someone who is' by 1 step, but keep the movement mini.

25.principle: Consistency comes from visibility rather than temperament.
25.science: Traceability and recording, self-monitoring, improve behavior at the % level (Harkin meta-analysis).
25.action: Leave a single line confirmation (date + icon) for today's action.

26.principle: Resisting resistance strengthens the inhibition muscle.
26.science: Inhibition control is strengthened by exercise, but depleted (ego depletion debates; don't forget to rest).
26.action: 1 deep breath + 1 repetition at trigger moment; disable thinking briefly.

27.principle: Appreciation is not by you, but by you.
27.science: Feeling of competence nourishes internal motivation; measure, see improvement (SDT).
27.action: Write yesterday's light or heavy exertion 1-10; no judgment, just data.

29.principle: The external reward can extinguish the instinct; the measure does not escape.
29.science: Risk of over-justification, uncontrolled external reinforcement (Lepper).
29.action: Return to the reward, complete signal: small, tangible, now.

30.principle: The question is no longer 'Did you do it?' but 'What version am I in?'
30.science: Identity-based execution aligns with self-reference sentences (self-perception, cognitive fit).
30.action: The same mini-variation for the third time under the name habit on day 30.

31.principle: Stress, good opportunity, bad excuse: the choice is yours.
31.science: Stress can disrupt automation; coping plans make a difference (implementation).
31.action: If the stress is 7+, make the version halfway, keep the attendance record.

32.principle: Recovery is a window to learning, not failure.
32.science: Incomplete version + short analysis makes the next attempt predictable (growth mindset, Dweck; by measurement).
32.action: If there was an abduction, 1 sentence: not the reason, 'tomorrow 30 sec' plan.

33.principle: The mind sells procrastination as' not painful now '.
33.science: Instant discounting underestimates future rewards; commitment device helps.
33.action: Leave a social/binding mini commitment to someone or place.

34.principle: The group breaks down selfishness; responsibility goes beyond reminding.
34.science: Accountability increases success rates (Cook & Jackson support studies).
34.action: Today, take the expectation of 'asked' or 'did you do it' from a person in 1 sentence.

35.principle: Resistance, like muscle: refuse to rest, you punch a hole.
35.science: Inhibition and self-regulation are limited; sleep and cognition inhibit exhaustion (Galliot glucose, discussions; summary: the fact of exhaustion).
35.action: In an exhaustion signal, the target is 10 sec/1 repetition, no sleep/sugar priority (before sleep).

36.principle: Mirroring, not; reminder: you talk to yourself.
36.science: Manages self-discourse, mood, and execution; healing language aligns with SDT.
36.action: Today, calm the inner voice with the 3rd person sentence, then 1 action.

37.principle: The line of progress is the triad of perception, measurement, and consistency.
37.science: Goal-gradient: speed when approaching the end; small milestones momentum (Hull, 1932 — modern interpretation).
37.action: Put 1 small flag in between: I'm in 37/44 reinforcement, 1 iteration today.

38.principle: The internal contradiction is resolved in mirrors, in how many it grows.
38.science: Cognitive contradiction, the need to reduce inconsistency, drives behavior (Festinger, measured interpretation).
38.action: One sentence: 'I, who do this, try this once' (alleviate the contradiction with 1 movement).

39.principle: The timeline is longer than the emotion; consistency is short, accumulates.
39.science: The exhausted day makes sense again on the next path, even 0.5 sec (the psychology of interruption).
39.action: Light day, 1 action anyway: duration no more than 10 s.

40.principle: Context is the exchange of clues, not punishment.
40.science: Stimulus control, new response with new cues; behavioral analysis.
40.action: List 1 bad environment variable, focus on 1 tomorrow.

41.principle: Internal rewards are learned satisfaction, not external dependence.
41.science: Internal reinforcement, advanced execution, carries long-term motivation (SDT).
41.action: After completion, a clear inner sentence: 'This is a small moment that defines me.'

42.principle: Not just procrastinate, but quit early; the two are separate pockets.
42.science: Stage model, intention-action distinction; recording the difference intervenes (Prochaska, in summary).
42.action: If it has been postponed, ban the sentence 'don't quit'; just limit the hours.

43.principle: Reinforcement last week: bottleneck, just your anchor.
43.science: Lally, arrival at vending machine range 18-254 days; 66, average, your speed is different (Lally).
43.action: Trigger you missed the most: Rename with 1 sentence (change name, same trigger).

44.principle: Phase 2 connected your identity phrases to the verb; phase 3 polishes the automaton.
44.science: When the habit becomes automatic, the burden of consciousness decreases (Wood).
44.action: Day 44: In 10 sec, 'where am I doing this without noticing?' 1 answer.

45.principle: You are no longer 'deciding', you are remembering and noticing.
45.science: Basal ganglia, automatics; prefrontal, handing over upper management (Willingham, procedural memory).
45.action: Today, notice 1 moment at the moment of trigger (silent, 2 sec).

46.science: Automation releases cognitive load; focus shifts to higher goals (dual-process).
46.action: Celebrate the automaton: 1 sentence, not 'I left this to me', but 'I leave this to me'.

47.principle: 66 days, average; your duration may be different, this is normal.
47.science: Lally variation: median/average distributed between 18-66+ weeks; person specific.
47.action: Every day in phase 3: record 'difference', not 'point now': headlessness/fluency 1-5.

48.principle: Boredom, not discipline, but the hissing of the system.
48.science: Routine is the line of uninterrupted repetition (habitization) rather than the wave of dopamine.
48.action: 1 sentence in his bored mind: 'Boredom, signal: I went into automation.'

49.principle: What touches identity is measured by unforgettable movement.
49.science: Self-schema updates are combined with consistent experimentation (cognitive-interpersonal acceptance).
49.action: To someone, or in the mirror, with a 5-second voice: 'I'm the one who did this.'

50.principle: Redesigning is not stopping, but moving forward.
50.science: Habit-continuity hypothesis: if the context changes, the habit stretches at the weak point (opportunity to intervene in the crisis).
50.action: Test 1 context variable: different time, same micro-action.

51.principle: Trust in automation, not letting go of control.
51.science: Target lines, fine play in meta-plane, slip prevention (Gollwitzer, if-then).
51.action: 1 if-then backup: 'If recording drops, same anchor on the same day in 30 sec.'

52.principle: The day of recovery is not the day of failure; it is the day of the signal.
52.science: Relapse prevention takes place by explaining the trigger and shrinking the plan (Marlatt).
52.action: Probability of deterioration: trigger+emotion 1 sentence; solution: mini if-then.

53.principle: Discipline is not punishment; it is deferred reward management.
53.science: Time preferences, procrastination, and self-control (Mischel, biscuit, summary: delayed reward).
53.action: No grand prize, small: today, 10 s of calm movement (stretch, etc.) upon completion.

54.science: Adaptation: new threshold after goal, habit stacking/goal hierarchy.
54.action: 1 sentence target draft for 67+: 'Same anchor, one upper 2 min.' (draft today).

55.principle: Day 55: signal, your speed, you can leave the average.
55.science: Lally distribution: person-specific variation, deviation from the mean is not extraordinary.
55.action: If heavy: 'I am a slow voyage' 1 sentence; 1 action again 2 min and below.

56.science: Implicit vs explicit procedural learning; reckless repetition (Willingham).
56.action: When the action is over, the silent 'what happened?' question for 3 seconds is non-judgmental.

57.principle: Contextual ache could be the signal for a new anchor trial in the last week.
57.science: Context-stimulative (renewal) risk, yet the planned re-dimension closes (summary).
57.action: Design 1 new, small anchor trial in 5 s, today just trial, no full performance.

58.principle: Not you, but the system: rest your ego, align the pattern.
58.science: System-1 automation, system-2 planning, system-1 wins in overlap, system-2 adjusts the environment (Stanovich).
58.action: Permanently correct 1 default in the environment (e.g. light, door, bag location).

59.principle: At 7 to 66, the record holds the mirror, not the boast.
59.science: Self-monitoring does not blind execution; reverse, eye-opening (Harkin).
59.action: Summarize the difference -1-5 trend since 47 in 1 sentence (if not, write 'data yet').

60.principle: Habit is the key that opens the next door, not the end.
60.science: Habit stacking, chain, trigger the previous one; Clear + behavior chain (Lally, easy when switching to automation).
60.action: 1 short, new, small 'after' 5 sec plan: +1 ring on top of existing line.

61.principle: If the community carried you in 66, leave it open in 67 or limit it —consciously.
61.science: Social benchmarking, extrinsic motivation, intrinsic weak influences (discussions, measure).
61.action: Account, share, or privacy in 1 sentence: which is the sustainer, today's choice.

62.principle: In the last 5, not the soul, but the rhythm: the ending does not play the rhythm, it carries it.
62.science: Closing effects, risk of acceleration (goal-gradient) and exhaustion in the target approach; rhythm, protective.
62.action: Today, the usual time, the usual place, the minimum variation, the maximum loyalty.

63.science: Cognitive simplification reduces over-planning in automation; minimalism accelerates execution (summary).
63.action: 1 item in the plan sneaks out, proceed as if nothing had happened (leave the driver at least).

65.principle: At 65, let go of continuity, not procrastination, to the next 66.
65.science: Continuity does not last with big new goals, but with expansion with the same core.
65.action: Same anchor + 1 must: tomorrow, just 1 minute, no difference, emphasis on continuation.

66.principle: This is not a running end, but completing a contract that promises automation.
66.science: Lally, individual variation in automation; 66, the symbol, the science person refuses to be peculiar 66 —but the ritual bridges.
66.action: 1 page or 1 sentence: Mark 'same/mini/art' from 67, just signature today.

daily: What single thing occupied your mind most today?
What are you thinking but haven't said out loud?
One small detail you're grateful for today?
One clear intention sentence for tomorrow?
Which thought kept you stuck today?
What's the smallest step you promised yourself today?
What do you feel in your body right now — one word?
What did you put off today, and why?
One sentence that reminds you who you are today?
Your energy is 1–10 today; if low, what can you shrink?
The moment you're proudest of today (even if small)?
One load you want to let go of right now?
When did you notice your anchor (trigger) today?
What will you do in the first 2 minutes tomorrow morning?
modal: What occupied my mind most today...
What I'm thinking but haven't said out loud...
A small detail I'm grateful for...
What I actually put off today...
The smallest step I promised myself...

1.title: When the brain is processing the new routine
1.body: In the first phase, the frontal shell is more vigilant: movement often requires conscious choice. The brain devotes “attention” to a new pattern; it's not fatigue, it's the price of learning. Small, finishable steps in the early days create less resistance than big plans.

1.title: Where does resistance come from?
1.body: The basal ganglia pattern is unclear when the habit is not yet automatic; that's why the body sometimes says “no.” Avoidance is normal. The goal is not zero avoidance — choosing the smallest return instead of guilt when a run is seen.

1.title: Small reward, big distance
1.body: In the early phase, the big reward comes infrequently; the brain may shift to short-term comfort. A small completed step increases the feeling of completion. Reduce today's gain to the body or one sentence — that's enough evidence for the long journey.

1.title: Sentence for establishment phase
1.body: In this phase, context and repeat wins: same row, same clue, same anchor. Your identity sentence should be short; you will give yourself "one vote" for each repetition. Discipline is the practice of reminding, not performance.

2.title: Intermediate period: boring but valuable
2.body: In the reinforcement phase, some movement becomes familiar; the boring feeling may increase. This isn't usually a weakness — it's a byproduct of automation. Boring days paint the map “colorless but steady.”

2.title: Context flexibility
2.body: When circumstances change, the brain looks for new clues. Being prepared for small environment changes (location, duration, sequence) is a savior in the days of decline. Start in “good enough” condition without waiting for the perfect condition.

2.title: Identity and repetition
2.body: In this phase, the sentence "I am someone who does this" becomes stronger as it is repeated. Attach the behavior to the person, but do not upload it like an exam — gentle repetition. If one day you are disrupted, the identity does not collapse with a single day; reconnecting is also part of the identity.

2.title: Sentence for reinforcement phase
2.body: Stability outlives inspiration. Motivation is wavy; routine is the wave-breaking scaffold. The one small move made today makes the rest of tomorrow more likely.

3.title: The gateway to automation
3.body: In the final phase, more repetition and context cohesion are in play; the brain's “I'll do it later” cost sometimes drops. This doesn't mean ’easy now‘ — it means getting closer to 'starting small‘.

3.title: Triggers and traps
3.body: Even the automated routine is shaken by stress or lack of sleep. Keeping known triggers in writing is protective: fatigue, congestion, social pressure. The plan is not highly exciting, let's get ready to fall.

3.title: Identity reinforcement
3.body: As you approach day 66, the goal isn't just to close a box — it's to align this behaviour with your sense of self. The more you feel 'I chose this', the more likely you are to keep going (close to subjective autonomy in SDT language).

3.title: A line for the automation phase
3.body: The goal is no longer a 'perfect day' but recovering quickly when you slip. The long road is the sum of small repairs. Even the smallest reconnection today carries you forward.

coachNotes.day7: The first week is behind you. The anchor is settling in — it gets easier now.
coachNotes.day14: Two weeks. New connections are forming in the brain. Even if you don't feel it.
coachNotes.day22: Critical point. Those who reach here usually finish.
coachNotes.day30: 30 days. The question is no longer 'are you doing it?' but 'who are you becoming?'
coachNotes.day44: Phase 2 is done. You're not choosing anymore — you're doing.
coachNotes.day66: 66 days. This is yours now — no one can take it.

checkInConfirmation.subtitle: Save quickly — you can think through the details later
